vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1818: Role-agent context-overflow handling for review convoys — fast-fail reviewers on 400 context errors, stop the generic patrol from mis-handling them, add a large-changeset reading guardrail, and reconcile CLIProxy window honesty.

plan.title: Review-role agents on gpt-5.5/claude-code (CLIProxy) die with 400 context errors — convoys lose a reviewer per cycle; no overflow recovery for role agents
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Reviewer sub-role agents running gpt-5.5 over claude-code via CLIProxy overflow the real upstream window while reading a big diff + 15-20 files and die on a 400. monitorReviewConvoySignals() never inspects the tail for the 400, so an overflowed reviewer waits 3 min idle then PAN-1806 respawns it identically (deterministic re-overflow) before failing — ~6+ min burned per reviewer. Worse, checkApiErrorAgents() treats the reviewer session as a work agent (issueId derived by stripping 'agent-'+uppercasing → garbage 'PAN-1815-REVIEW-CORRECTNESS') and races the monitor with work-agent compact-respawn. The gpt-5.5 capability notes also contradict its own 150k window. Net effect: every failed reviewer forces a full re-review cycle and blocks the work agent 12+ min.
narratives.Proposal: Three surgical, harness-independent changes. (1) Fast-fail reviewers on overflow: monitorReviewConvoySignals captures the reviewer tail and, on a context-overflow match with no output written, signals REVIEWER_FAILED immediately without respawning; and checkApiErrorAgents excludes convoy reviewer sub-role sessions so the monitor is their sole owner. (2) Add a large-changeset signal to the review manifest and a conditional selective-reading guardrail in the Tier-1 summary so reviewers facing a huge diff read narrowly and degrade to a coverage-flagged report instead of overflowing. (3) Reconcile the gpt-5.5 notes drift and lock the 150k conservative ceiling with a test. No chunked-review rearchitecture; no changes to retry counts, harness defaults, the ToS gate, or in-flight-guard.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Rearchitecting review into chunked / multi-pass reviewers (reference-by-path already exists via PAN-1125).
- Generalizing the conservative CLIProxy ceiling to all routed models inside computeContextUsage() (conversation-side window honesty owned by PAN-1672).
- Fixing the synthesis agent's (agent-<issue>-review) garbage-issueId derivation in checkApiErrorAgents — WI-2 excludes only reviewer sub-role sessions.
- Changing convoy retry counts / deadlines (#1806) or the openai-model harness default (PAN-1787/PAN-1797).
- Touching canUseHarnessSync (ToS gate) or the in-flight-guard idempotency test.

autoDecisions.summary: Ask #1 (cap inlined diff) treated as already-done by PAN-1125; address only residual runtime-read overflow with a lightweight guardrail, not chunking.
autoDecisions.rationale: Reviewer prompts already inline only a Tier-1 summary, never the raw diff (review-context.ts:25-27). Rebuilding shipped behavior violates simplicity-first.

autoDecisions.summary: On reviewer overflow, fast-fail without respawn (not respawn-once like #1806).
autoDecisions.rationale: Overflow on the same manifest is deterministic; respawning re-overflows and burns ~6 min. #1806's respawn is correct only for transient API errors.

autoDecisions.summary: Fix checkApiErrorAgents by excluding reviewer sub-role sessions, not by correcting the issueId derivation.
autoDecisions.rationale: A reviewer must never receive work-agent compact-respawn (bd/continue-seeded). Single-owner lifecycle (monitorReviewConvoySignals) avoids the race.

autoDecisions.summary: Ask #3 scoped to notes-drift fix + lock test; defer ceiling generalization to PAN-1672.
autoDecisions.rationale: gpt-5.5 already keys proactive compaction off 150k; generalization is conversation-side PAN-1672 work, and a 60s patrol can't catch a reviewer's fast read-burst overflow anyway.

autoDecisions.summary: Large-changeset threshold = >25 files OR >1500 changed lines, as named constants.
autoDecisions.rationale: Evidence PR #1815 was ~3225 changed lines / 24 items; threshold triggers well below that and above ordinary PRs, and stays tunable.

items.title: Fast-fail convoy reviewers on context overflow in monitorReviewConvoySignals (no respawn)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts monitorReviewConvoySignals() (~:5788), after outputWrittenForThisRun and reviewerSessionAlive are computed and BEFORE the existing signal if/else ladder (~:5862), add an overflow check: when !outputWrittenForThisRun, capture the reviewer pane tail via await Effect.runPromise(capturePane(agentId, 100)) (already imported at :156) and test with isContextOverflowTail() (import from '../context-overflow.js'). On match, set signal='failed', reason='context-window overflow (no retry — deterministic)', reaching this branch before the idleAndNoOutput/respawnIdleReviewer branch (~:5868) so an overflowed reviewer never respawns. Leave REVIEWER_FAILED message construction and reviewMonitorSignaled write unchanged.

items.title: Given a convoy reviewer that is alive, has written no output for this run, and whose pane tail contains 'input exceeds the context window', when monitorReviewConvoySignals runs, then it sends the synthesis agent REVIEWER_FAILED <subRole> with a reason naming 'context-window overflow'.
Given the same overflowed reviewer, when monitorReviewConvoySignals runs, then respawnIdleReviewer is NOT invoked (no respawn for deterministic overflow).

Given a reviewer that is idle with no output but whose tail shows NO overflow pattern, when monitorReviewConvoySignals runs, then the existing PAN-1806 idle-respawn path is preserved (respawnIdleReviewer still invoked on first idle detection).
Given an overflowed reviewer, the monitor emits the REVIEWER_FAILED signal on the first pass that observes the overflow tail, without waiting for the 3-minute REVIEWER_IDLE_FAILURE_MS idle threshold.
Exclude convoy reviewer sub-role sessions from checkApiErrorAgents generic recovery

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents() (~:964), skip sessions matching the convoy reviewer pattern /^agent-.*-review-(?:security|correctness|performance|requirements)$/ (the same regex used by cleanupOrphanedReviewSessions at :5957) — either by filtering them out of agentSessions (:977-979) or continuing at the top of the per-session loop. Add a one-line comment (PAN-1818) that monitorReviewConvoySignals() is the sole lifecycle owner of these sessions. This prevents the garbage-issueId work-agent recovery (resumeAgent compact-respawn / markWorkspaceStuck) from acting on reviewers and racing the monitor. Do NOT change handling of the synthesis session (agent-<issue>-review) or any work/planning session.

items.title: Given a session named agent-pan-1815-review-correctness whose pane tail shows a context-overflow 400, when checkApiErrorAgents runs, then it performs NO recovery action on it: resumeAgent, markWorkspaceStuck, and sendKeys are never called for that session.

Given a normal work-agent session agent-pan-1815 with a context-overflow 400, when checkApiErrorAgents runs, then its existing recovery still fires (regression guard that the exclusion is scoped to reviewer sub-role sessions only).
Add large-changeset signal to review manifest + conditional selective-reading guardrail in Tier-1 summary

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/review-context.ts: add module constants REVIEW_LARGE_CHANGESET_FILES=25 and REVIEW_LARGE_CHANGESET_LINES=1500; add a largeChangeset field { fileCount: number; changedLines: number; isLarge: boolean } to ReviewContextManifest (:67) computed in the manifest build (fileCount=changedFiles.length, changedLines=sum of additions+deletions, isLarge=fileCount>FILES || changedLines>LINES). In formatTier1Summary() (~:331), when manifest.largeChangeset.isLarge, append a guidance block instructing the reviewer to read the highest-risk files first, read only changed regions (not whole files), and if approaching the context limit write a report on what was reviewed and flag the uncovered high-risk files as a BLOCKING coverage gap rather than reading everything and overflowing. When not large, append nothing.

items.title: Given a changeset of >25 files or >1500 total changed lines, when buildReviewContext runs, then manifest.largeChangeset.isLarge === true with correct fileCount and changedLines.

Given a large changeset manifest, when formatTier1Summary runs, then its output contains the selective-reading guardrail block (mentions reading highest-risk files first and flagging uncovered files as a blocking coverage gap).
Given a small changeset (<=25 files and <=1500 lines), when formatTier1Summary runs, then isLarge === false and the guardrail block is absent (small-PR prompts unchanged).
Reconcile gpt-5.5 capability notes with its 150k CLIProxy ceiling and lock with a test

narrative.Action: In src/lib/model-capabilities.ts gpt-5.5 entry (:460-481): replace the contradictory notes clause 'Effective Claude Code/CLIProxy ceiling is 200K, 128K max output.' with text consistent with the 150k conservative ceiling, e.g. 'Routed via CLIProxy; conservative effective context ceiling 150K (CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW, PAN-1672), 128K max output.' Do NOT change contextWindow (already CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW) or any skill score. Add a test asserting MODEL_CAPABILITIES['gpt-5.5'].contextWindow === CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW (new model-capabilities unit test, or extend conversation-service.test.ts which already references the 150k window at :111).

items.title: Given the gpt-5.5 capability entry, then its notes no longer claim a 200K effective ceiling and instead state the 150K conservative CLIProxy ceiling.
A unit test preserves the invariant that MODEL_CAPABILITIES['gpt-5.5'].contextWindow equals CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW, failing if the two ever diverge.
Given the change, then MODEL_CAPABILITIES['gpt-5.5'].contextWindow still equals 150000 and no gpt-5.5 skill score is modified (notes-only edit, value preserved).
